channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. Lett.
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review Letters
channel.dc:creator: rss@aps.org

item.title: [8] Observation of a Pronounced Hebel-Slichter Peak in the Spin-Lattice Relaxation Rate and Implications for Gap and Pairing Symmetry in LaNiGa2
item.description:
Last authors: P. Klavins, R. R. P. Singh, V. Taufour, and N. J. Curro

Authors: P. Sherpa, R. Hingorani, A. Menon, I. Vinograd, C. Chaffey, A. P. Dioguardi, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental NQR study of a topological crystalline superconductor, relevant to unconventional and topological superconductivity.

Tags: #superconductivity #topological #NMR/NQR

Abstract: Author(s): P. Sherpa, R. Hingorani, A. Menon, I. Vinograd, C. Chaffey, A. P. Dioguardi, R. Yamamoto, M. Hirata, F. Ronning, J. R. Badger, P. Klavins, R. R. P. Singh, V. Taufour, and N. J. Curro We report a pronounced Hebel-Slichter coherence peak in the zero field nuclear quadrupolar resonance (NQR) spin-lattice relaxation rate of the topological crystalline superconductor ${\mathrm{LaNiGa}}_{2}$ in the superconducting state. Previously, a two-band internally antisymmetric nonunitary tripl… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 186001] Published Wed May 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): P. Sherpa, R. Hingorani, A. Menon, I. Vinograd, C. Chaffey, A. P. Dioguardi, R. Yamamoto, M. Hirata, F. Ronning, J. R. Badger, P. Klavins, R. R. P. Singh, V. Taufour, and N. J. Curro

We report a pronounced Hebel-Slichter coherence peak in the zero field nuclear quadrupolar resonance (NQR) spin-lattice relaxation rate of the topological crystalline superconductor LaNiGa2 in the superconducting state. Previously, a two-band internally antisymmetric nonunitary triplet pairing state…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 186001] Published Wed May 06, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Observation of a Pronounced Hebel-Slichter Peak in the Spin-Lattice Relaxation Rate and Implications for Gap and Pairing Symmetry in LaNiGa2
item.dc:creator: P. Sherpa, R. Hingorani, A. Menon, I. Vinograd, C. Chaffey, A. P. Dioguardi, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [8] Distinct Behaviors of Inner and Outer CuO2 Planes in Quadruple-Layer Cuprate (Cu,C)Ba2Ca3Cu4O11+δ
item.description:
Last authors: Rui Peng, Donglai Feng

Authors: Xingtian Sun, Suppanut Sangphet, Nan Guo, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Direct electronic structure and superconducting gap measurements in a quadruple-layer cuprate.

Tags: #superconductivity #cuprates #electronicstructure

Abstract: Author(s): Xingtian Sun, Suppanut Sangphet, Nan Guo, Yu Fan, Yutong Chen, Minyinan Lei, Xue Ming, Xiyu Zhu, Hai-Hu Wen, Haichao Xu, Rui Peng, and Donglai Feng The superconducting transition temperatures (${T}_{\mathrm{c}}$’s) of trilayer or quadruple-layer cuprates typically surpass those of single-layer or bilayer systems. However, the lack of direct electronic-structure and superconducting-gap measurements in optimal-${T}_{\mathrm{c}}$ quadruple-layer c… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 186401] Published Wed May 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xingtian Sun, Suppanut Sangphet, Nan Guo, Yu Fan, Yutong Chen, Minyinan Lei, Xue Ming, Xiyu Zhu, Hai-Hu Wen, Haichao Xu, Rui Peng, and Donglai Feng

The superconducting transition temperatures (Tc’s) of trilayer or quadruple-layer cuprates typically surpass those of single-layer or bilayer systems. However, the lack of direct electronic-structure and superconducting-gap measurements in optimal-Tc quadruple-layer cuprates has impeded a comprehens…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 186401] Published Wed May 06, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Distinct Behaviors of Inner and Outer CuO2 Planes in Quadruple-Layer Cuprate (Cu,C)Ba2Ca3Cu4O11+δ
item.dc:creator: Xingtian Sun, Suppanut Sangphet, Nan Guo, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [8] Thermoelectricity of Moiré Heavy Fermions in MoTe2/WSe2 Bilayers
item.description:
Last authors: Jie Shan, Kin Fai Mak

Authors: Yichi Zhang, Wenjin Zhao, Zhongdong Han, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study of thermoelectricity in moiré heavy fermions in a twisted bilayer system.

Tags: #moire #heavyfermion #2Dmaterials #thermoelectricity

Abstract: Author(s): Yichi Zhang, Wenjin Zhao, Zhongdong Han, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Jie Shan, and Kin Fai Mak Tunable Kondo lattice and heavy fermion physics have been recently reported in moiré materials, but most of the studies have focused on the electrical and magnetic properties. Quantitative thermoelectric measurements, which can reveal entropic information of the heavy fermions, have yet to be achiev… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 186501] Published Wed May 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yichi Zhang, Wenjin Zhao, Zhongdong Han, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Jie Shan, and Kin Fai Mak

Tunable Kondo lattice and heavy fermion physics have been recently reported in moiré materials, but most of the studies have focused on the electrical and magnetic properties. Quantitative thermoelectric measurements, which can reveal entropic information of the heavy fermions, have yet to be achiev…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 186501] Published Wed May 06, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Thermoelectricity of Moiré Heavy Fermions in MoTe2/WSe2 Bilayers
item.dc:creator: Yichi Zhang, Wenjin Zhao, Zhongdong Han, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [7] Quantum-Geometric Dipole: A Topological Boost to Flavor Ferromagnetism in Flat Bands
item.description:
Last authors: Jennifer Cano, Valentin Crépel

Authors: Lei Chen, Sayed Ali Akbar Ghorashi, Jennifer Cano, Valentin Crépel

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory on flavor ferromagnetism in moiré flat bands, connecting quantum geometry to topology and magnetism.

Tags: #moire #flatband #topological #ferromagnetism #theory

Abstract: Author(s): Lei Chen, Sayed Ali Akbar Ghorashi, Jennifer Cano, and Valentin Crépel Robust flavor-polarized phases are a striking hallmark of many flat-band moiré materials. In this Letter, we trace the origin of this spontaneous polarization to a lesser-known quantum-geometric quantity: the quantum-geometric dipole . Analogous to how the quantum metric governs the spatial spread of… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 186602] Published Wed May 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Lei Chen, Sayed Ali Akbar Ghorashi, Jennifer Cano, and Valentin Crépel

Robust flavor-polarized phases are a striking hallmark of many flat-band moiré materials. In this Letter, we trace the origin of this spontaneous polarization to a lesser-known quantum-geometric quantity: the quantum-geometric dipole. Analogous to how the quantum metric governs the spatial spread of…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 186602] Published Wed May 06, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Quantum-Geometric Dipole: A Topological Boost to Flavor Ferromagnetism in Flat Bands
item.dc:creator: Lei Chen, Sayed Ali Akbar Ghorashi, Jennifer Cano, Valentin Crépel
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [9] Altermagnetic Proximity Effect
item.description:
Last authors: Igor Žutić, Tong Zhou

Authors: Ziye Zhu, Richang Huang, Xianzhang Chen, et al.

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Directly relevant to user's interest in altermagnetism, albeit a theory paper.

Tags: #altermagnetism

Abstract: Author(s): Ziye Zhu, Richang Huang, Xianzhang Chen, Zhou Cui, Xunkai Duan, Jiayong Zhang, Igor Žutić, and Tong Zhou According to theory, a property called altermagnetism can be acquired by a nonmagnetic material that is adjacent to an altermagnet. [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 186702] Published Tue May 05, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ziye Zhu, Richang Huang, Xianzhang Chen, Zhou Cui, Xunkai Duan, Jiayong Zhang, Igor Žutić, and Tong Zhou

According to theory, a property called altermagnetism can be acquired by a nonmagnetic material that is adjacent to an altermagnet.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 186702] Published Tue May 05, 2026
item.dc:title: [9] Altermagnetic Proximity Effect
item.dc:creator: Ziye Zhu, Richang Huang, Xianzhang Chen, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [7] Quantum Spin Liquid Phase in the Shastry-Sutherland Model Revealed by High-Precision Infinite Projected Entangled-Pair States
item.description:
Last authors: Boris Ponsioen, Frédéric Mila

Authors: Philippe Corboz, Yining Zhang, Boris Ponsioen, Frédéric Mila

Score 7/10 C_MAYBE

Why: Theory on quantum spin liquids, a relevant topic for strongly correlated systems.; tier capped to C: formal/generic theory watch

Tags: #quantumspinliquid #stronglycorrelatedelectronsystems

Abstract: Author(s): Philippe Corboz, Yining Zhang, Boris Ponsioen, and Frédéric Mila High-precision tensor network calculations in the 2D thermodynamic limit reveal a narrow quantum spin liquid phase, consistent with previous studies, but based on variational states significantly closer to the exact ground state in the thermodynamic limit. [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 186701] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Philippe Corboz, Yining Zhang, Boris Ponsioen, and Frédéric Mila

High-precision tensor network calculations in the 2D thermodynamic limit reveal a narrow quantum spin liquid phase, consistent with previous studies, but based on variational states significantly closer to the exact ground state in the thermodynamic limit.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 186701] Published Mon May 04, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Quantum Spin Liquid Phase in the Shastry-Sutherland Model Revealed by High-Precision Infinite Projected Entangled-Pair States
item.dc:creator: Philippe Corboz, Yining Zhang, Boris Ponsioen, Frédéric Mila
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [8] Magnetic-Field-Driven Insulator-Superconductor Transition in Rhombohedral Graphene
item.description:
Last authors: Xi Lin, Xiaobo Lu

Authors: Jian Xie, Zihao Huo, Zhimou Chen, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study of unconventional superconductivity in graphene, highly relevant.

Tags: #graphene #unconventionalsuperconductivity

Abstract: Author(s): Jian Xie, Zihao Huo, Zhimou Chen, Zaizhe Zhang, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Xi Lin, and Xiaobo Lu Recent studies of rhombohedral multilayer graphene have revealed a variety of superconducting states that can be induced or enhanced by magnetic fields, reinforcing rhombohedral multilayer graphene as a powerful platform for investigating novel superconductivity. Here, we report an insulator-superco… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176505] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jian Xie, Zihao Huo, Zhimou Chen, Zaizhe Zhang, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Xi Lin, and Xiaobo Lu

Recent studies of rhombohedral multilayer graphene have revealed a variety of superconducting states that can be induced or enhanced by magnetic fields, reinforcing rhombohedral multilayer graphene as a powerful platform for investigating novel superconductivity. Here, we report an insulator-superco…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176505] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Magnetic-Field-Driven Insulator-Superconductor Transition in Rhombohedral Graphene
item.dc:creator: Jian Xie, Zihao Huo, Zhimou Chen, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [8] Microwave Imaging of Edge Conductivity in Graphene at Charge Neutrality and Quantum Hall States
item.description:
Last authors: Matthew Yankowitz, Keji Lai

Authors: Hongtao Yan, Chun-Chih Tseng, Anzhuoer Li, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental paper on graphene, quantum Hall states, and topological/magnetic order.

Tags: #graphene #quantumHall #topological #antiferromagnet

Abstract: Author(s): Hongtao Yan, Chun-Chih Tseng, Anzhuoer Li, Manish Kumar, Kaile Wang, Shizai Chu, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Allan H. MacDonald, Matthew Yankowitz, and Keji Lai The contrasting edge evolution between ν = 0 , consistent with a canted antiferromagnetic phase, and | ν | ≥ 1 chiral edge channels reveals the microscopic distinction between trivial and topological states. [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176603] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hongtao Yan, Chun-Chih Tseng, Anzhuoer Li, Manish Kumar, Kaile Wang, Shizai Chu, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Allan H. MacDonald, Matthew Yankowitz, and Keji Lai

The contrasting edge evolution between ν=0, consistent with a canted antiferromagnetic phase, and |ν|≥1 chiral edge channels reveals the microscopic distinction between trivial and topological states.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176603] Published Fri May 01, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Microwave Imaging of Edge Conductivity in Graphene at Charge Neutrality and Quantum Hall States
item.dc:creator: Hongtao Yan, Chun-Chih Tseng, Anzhuoer Li, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [8] Strain-Controlled Atomic Reconstruction and Quasi-1D Excitons in Moiré Heterostructures
item.description:
Last authors: Anvar S. Baimuratov, Alexander Högele

Authors: Shen Zhao, Zhijie Li, Zakhar A. Iakovlev, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study on quasi-1D excitons in moiré heterostructures, relevant to user's interests.

Tags: #quasi-1D #moiré #heterostructures #2Dmaterials

Abstract: Author(s): Shen Zhao, Zhijie Li, Zakhar A. Iakovlev, Peirui Ji, Tao Jiang, Fanrong Lin, Xin Huang, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Mikhail M. Glazov, Anvar S. Baimuratov, and Alexander Högele In two-dimensional materials, strain provides effective means for tailoring electronic and optical properties. While uni- or biaxial strain has been widely implemented in monolayer semiconductors, deterministic control over atomic reconstruction and the resulting microscopic stacking textures in moi… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176201] Published Thu Apr 30, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shen Zhao, Zhijie Li, Zakhar A. Iakovlev, Peirui Ji, Tao Jiang, Fanrong Lin, Xin Huang, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Mikhail M. Glazov, Anvar S. Baimuratov, and Alexander Högele

In two-dimensional materials, strain provides effective means for tailoring electronic and optical properties. While uni- or biaxial strain has been widely implemented in monolayer semiconductors, deterministic control over atomic reconstruction and the resulting microscopic stacking textures in moi…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176201] Published Thu Apr 30, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Strain-Controlled Atomic Reconstruction and Quasi-1D Excitons in Moiré Heterostructures
item.dc:creator: Shen Zhao, Zhijie Li, Zakhar A. Iakovlev, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [7] Magnetism Induced by Periodically Driven Nonmagnetic Impurities on Surfaces with Spin-Orbit Coupling
item.description:
Last authors: Andrés Arnau, Asier Eiguren

Authors: Malen Etxeberria-Etxaniz, Andrés Arnau, Asier Eiguren

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory on Floquet-induced magnetism on Rashba surfaces, relevant to spin-orbit coupling and experiments.

Tags: #Floquetengineering #spin-orbitcoupling #magnetism #surfaces

Abstract: Author(s): Malen Etxeberria-Etxaniz, Andrés Arnau, and Asier Eiguren We investigate the response of the Rashba spin-orbit system to a time-periodic scalar potential, in order to determine whether an induced magnetization exists. We approach this by employing the Floquet Green’s function method within the Keldysh formalism, computing the nonequilibrium steady state of… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176401] Published Thu Apr 30, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Malen Etxeberria-Etxaniz, Andrés Arnau, and Asier Eiguren

We investigate the response of the Rashba spin-orbit system to a time-periodic scalar potential, in order to determine whether an induced magnetization exists. We approach this by employing the Floquet Green’s function method within the Keldysh formalism, computing the nonequilibrium steady state of…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176401] Published Thu Apr 30, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Magnetism Induced by Periodically Driven Nonmagnetic Impurities on Surfaces with Spin-Orbit Coupling
item.dc:creator: Malen Etxeberria-Etxaniz, Andrés Arnau, Asier Eiguren
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [7] Identifying Instabilities with Quantum Geometry in Flat-Band Systems
item.description:
Last authors: Leon Balents, Lucile Savary

Authors: Jia-Xin Zhang, Wen O. Wang, Leon Balents, Lucile Savary

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory of instabilities in flat-band systems using quantum geometry, relevant to correlated materials.

Tags: #flatband #quantumgeometry #stronglycorrelated #electronicorder

Abstract: Author(s): Jia-Xin Zhang, Wen O. Wang, Leon Balents, and Lucile Savary Despite the absence of a well-defined Fermi surface in flat-band systems, a nesting structure related to a geometric quantity constructed from the order parameter and the projection operator onto flat-band states replaces Fermi-surface nesting and determines the most favorable ordering across an arbitrary number of flat bands. [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176504] Published Thu Apr 30, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jia-Xin Zhang, Wen O. Wang, Leon Balents, and Lucile Savary

Despite the absence of a well-defined Fermi surface in flat-band systems, a nesting structure related to a geometric quantity constructed from the order parameter and the projection operator onto flat-band states replaces Fermi-surface nesting and determines the most favorable ordering across an arbitrary number of flat bands.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176504] Published Thu Apr 30, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Identifying Instabilities with Quantum Geometry in Flat-Band Systems
item.dc:creator: Jia-Xin Zhang, Wen O. Wang, Leon Balents, Lucile Savary
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [9] Spectroscopic Evidence of Disorder-Induced Quantum Phase Transitions in Monolayer Fe(Te,Se) Superconductor
item.description:
Last authors: Nandini Trivedi, Jian Wang

Authors: Guanyang He, Ziqiao Wang, Longxin Pan, et al.

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Spectroscopic evidence of quantum phase transitions in a monolayer iron-based superconductor, highly relevant.; demoted from A: broad CM, not direct user/project hit

Tags: #spectroscopy #superconductivity #monolayer #quantumphasetransition

Abstract: Author(s): Guanyang He, Ziqiao Wang, Longxin Pan, Yuxuan Lei, Fa Wang, Yi Liu, Nandini Trivedi, and Jian Wang The superconductor-insulator transition as a paradigm of quantum phase transitions has attracted tremendous interest over the past three decades. While the magnetic field and carrier density can be tuned to drive the transition, the role of disorder in the transition is not well understood due to th… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176001] Published Wed Apr 29, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Guanyang He, Ziqiao Wang, Longxin Pan, Yuxuan Lei, Fa Wang, Yi Liu, Nandini Trivedi, and Jian Wang

The superconductor-insulator transition as a paradigm of quantum phase transitions has attracted tremendous interest over the past three decades. While the magnetic field and carrier density can be tuned to drive the transition, the role of disorder in the transition is not well understood due to th…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176001] Published Wed Apr 29, 2026
item.dc:title: [9] Spectroscopic Evidence of Disorder-Induced Quantum Phase Transitions in Monolayer Fe(Te,Se) Superconductor
item.dc:creator: Guanyang He, Ziqiao Wang, Longxin Pan, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [7] Topological Robustness of Anyon Tunneling at ν=1/3
item.description:
Last authors: Claudio Chamon, Michael Manfra

Authors: Adithya Suresh, Ramon Guerrero-Suarez, Tanmay Maiti, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Important condensed matter; fractional quantum Hall and Luttinger liquid are relevant.

Tags: #Luttingerliquid #quantumHall #topological

Abstract: Author(s): Adithya Suresh, Ramon Guerrero-Suarez, Tanmay Maiti, Shuang Liang, Geoffrey Gardner, Claudio Chamon, and Michael Manfra In a chiral Luttinger liquid the scaling exponent for the 1/3 edge mode is insensitive to perturbations within the incompressible fractional quantum Hall effect state and establishes the bulk-boundary correspondence for topologically ordered states. [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176602] Published Wed Apr 29, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Adithya Suresh, Ramon Guerrero-Suarez, Tanmay Maiti, Shuang Liang, Geoffrey Gardner, Claudio Chamon, and Michael Manfra

In a chiral Luttinger liquid the scaling exponent for the 1/3 edge mode is insensitive to perturbations within the incompressible fractional quantum Hall effect state and establishes the bulk-boundary correspondence for topologically ordered states.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176602] Published Wed Apr 29, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Topological Robustness of Anyon Tunneling at ν=1/3
item.dc:creator: Adithya Suresh, Ramon Guerrero-Suarez, Tanmay Maiti, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [8] Spin Waves Excited by Hard X-Ray Transient Gratings
item.description:
Last authors: Peter R. Miedaner et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study using X-rays to excite and probe spin waves, relevant to magnetic dynamics.

Tags: #X-rayspectroscopy #magneticdynamics #spinwaves #instrumentation

Abstract: Author(s): Peter R. Miedaner et al. Recent progress in ultrafast x-ray sources helped establish x-rays as an important tool for probing lattice and magnetic dynamics initiated by femtosecond optical pulses. Here, we explore the potential of ultrashort hard x-ray pulses for driving magnetic dynamics. We use a transient grating techniqu… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176701] Published Wed Apr 29, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Peter R. Miedaner et al.

Recent progress in ultrafast x-ray sources helped establish x-rays as an important tool for probing lattice and magnetic dynamics initiated by femtosecond optical pulses. Here, we explore the potential of ultrashort hard x-ray pulses for driving magnetic dynamics. We use a transient grating techniqu…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176701] Published Wed Apr 29, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Spin Waves Excited by Hard X-Ray Transient Gratings
item.dc:creator: Peter R. Miedaner et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [7] Coherent Subgap Transport in Spin-Split Josephson Junctions
item.description:
Last authors: F. Sebastian Bergeret, Alfredo Levy Yeyati

Authors: David Caldevilla-Asenjo, Gorm Ole Steffensen, Sara Catalano, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental coherent subgap transport in ferromagnetic-superconductor junctions relevant to superconductivity and magnetism.

Tags: #superconductivity #magnetism #transport

Abstract: Author(s): David Caldevilla-Asenjo, Gorm Ole Steffensen, Sara Catalano, Alberto Hijano, Maxim Ilyn, Celia Rogero, Ramon Aguado, F. Sebastian Bergeret, and Alfredo Levy Yeyati We report the first experimental observation of subgap transport in ferromagnetic insulator–superconductor–insulator–superconductor junctions realized in $\mathrm{EuS}/\mathrm{Al}/\mathrm{AlOx}/\mathrm{Al}$ vertical stacks. Differential conductance measurements reveal multiple Andreev reflection pea… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176301] Published Tue Apr 28, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): David Caldevilla-Asenjo, Gorm Ole Steffensen, Sara Catalano, Alberto Hijano, Maxim Ilyn, Celia Rogero, Ramon Aguado, F. Sebastian Bergeret, and Alfredo Levy Yeyati

We report the first experimental observation of subgap transport in ferromagnetic insulator–superconductor–insulator–superconductor junctions realized in EuS/Al/AlOx/Al vertical stacks. Differential conductance measurements reveal multiple Andreev reflection peaks, with odd-order peaks split by the …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176301] Published Tue Apr 28, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Coherent Subgap Transport in Spin-Split Josephson Junctions
item.dc:creator: David Caldevilla-Asenjo, Gorm Ole Steffensen, Sara Catalano, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [8] Superconductivity of Incoherent Electrons near the Relativistic Mott Transition in Twisted Dirac Materials
item.description:
Last authors: Daniel E. Sheehy, Jörg Schmalian

Authors: Veronika C. Stangier, Mathias S. Scheurer, Daniel E. Sheehy, Jörg Schmalian

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory of superconductivity in twisted Dirac/2D materials near Mott transition, highly relevant.

Tags: #superconductivity #Mott #twistedbilayer #Diracmaterials #graphene #stronglycorrelated

Abstract: Author(s): Veronika C. Stangier, Mathias S. Scheurer, Daniel E. Sheehy, and Jörg Schmalian Superconductivity emerges in an unexpected way in twisted two-dimensional materials like graphene, even when there are essentially no mobile charge carriers at zero temperature. [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176501] Published Tue Apr 28, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Veronika C. Stangier, Mathias S. Scheurer, Daniel E. Sheehy, and Jörg Schmalian

Superconductivity emerges in an unexpected way in twisted two-dimensional materials like graphene, even when there are essentially no mobile charge carriers at zero temperature.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176501] Published Tue Apr 28, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Superconductivity of Incoherent Electrons near the Relativistic Mott Transition in Twisted Dirac Materials
item.dc:creator: Veronika C. Stangier, Mathias S. Scheurer, Daniel E. Sheehy, Jörg Schmalian
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [8] Emergent Topology from Landau Level Mixing in Quantum Hall-Superconductor Nanostructures
item.description:
Last authors: Alfredo Levy Yeyati, Pablo Burset

Authors: Yuriko Baba, Alfredo Levy Yeyati, Pablo Burset

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Combines quantum Hall and superconductivity to generate topological phases, fitting broader user interests.

Tags: #topological #superconductors #quantumHall

Abstract: Author(s): Yuriko Baba, Alfredo Levy Yeyati, and Pablo Burset We demonstrate the emergence of novel topological phases in quantum Hall-superconductor hybrid structures driven by Landau-level mixing and spin-orbit coupling. For a narrow superconducting stripe atop a two-dimensional electron gas, hybridization of chiral Andreev edge states yields a rich phase di… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176601] Published Mon Apr 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yuriko Baba, Alfredo Levy Yeyati, and Pablo Burset

We demonstrate the emergence of novel topological phases in quantum Hall-superconductor hybrid structures driven by Landau-level mixing and spin-orbit coupling. For a narrow superconducting stripe atop a two-dimensional electron gas, hybridization of chiral Andreev edge states yields a rich phase di…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 176601] Published Mon Apr 27, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Emergent Topology from Landau Level Mixing in Quantum Hall-Superconductor Nanostructures
item.dc:creator: Yuriko Baba, Alfredo Levy Yeyati, Pablo Burset

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [8] Elastic Response and Instabilities of Anomalous Hall Crystals
item.description:
Last authors: T. Senthil, Yong Baek Kim

Authors: Félix Desrochers, Mark R. Hirsbrunner, Joe Huxford, Adarsh S. Patri, T. Senthil, Yong Baek Kim

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Discusses anomalous Hall effect and quantum anomalous Hall in graphene systems, relevant to user's broader interests.

Tags: #anomalousHall #graphene

Abstract: Author(s): Félix Desrochers, Mark R. Hirsbrunner, Joe Huxford, Adarsh S. Patri, T. Senthil, and Yong Baek Kim Anomalous Hall crystals (AHCs) are exotic phases of matter that simultaneously break continuous translation symmetry and exhibit the quantum anomalous Hall effect. AHCs have recently been proposed to explain the observation of an integer quantum anomalous Hall phase in a multilayer graphene system. … [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166503] Published Fri Apr 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Félix Desrochers, Mark R. Hirsbrunner, Joe Huxford, Adarsh S. Patri, T. Senthil, and Yong Baek Kim

Anomalous Hall crystals (AHCs) are exotic phases of matter that simultaneously break continuous translation symmetry and exhibit the quantum anomalous Hall effect. AHCs have recently been proposed to explain the observation of an integer quantum anomalous Hall phase in a multilayer graphene system. …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166503] Published Fri Apr 24, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Elastic Response and Instabilities of Anomalous Hall Crystals
item.dc:creator: Félix Desrochers, Mark R. Hirsbrunner, Joe Huxford, Adarsh S. Patri, T. Senthil, Yong Baek Kim
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [8] Ultrafast Moiré-Resolved Spectroscopy of Interlayer-Exciton Thermalization in Twisted WSe2/WS2 Heterobilayers
item.description:
Last authors: Tony Heinz, Hyunyong Choi

Authors: Jinjae Kim, Hyeonho Lee, Claudia Gollner, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Ultrafast spectroscopy on moiré twisted bilayer heterostructures, strong correlation physics, relevant to user's broader interests.

Tags: #moire #twistedbilayer #vanderWaalsheterostructures #spectroscopy #stronglycorrelated

Abstract: Author(s): Jinjae Kim, Hyeonho Lee, Claudia Gollner, Yannick Pleimling, Sumin Lee, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Moon-Ho Jo, Tony Heinz, and Hyunyong Choi Interlayer excitons (IXs) in transition metal dichalcogenide moiré heterobilayers offer a versatile platform for exploring strongly correlated quantum phases. Despite recent progress in probing excitonic properties, the thermalization dynamics of IXs, particularly within moiré superlattices, remain … [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166902] Published Fri Apr 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jinjae Kim, Hyeonho Lee, Claudia Gollner, Yannick Pleimling, Sumin Lee, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Moon-Ho Jo, Tony Heinz, and Hyunyong Choi

Interlayer excitons (IXs) in transition metal dichalcogenide moiré heterobilayers offer a versatile platform for exploring strongly correlated quantum phases. Despite recent progress in probing excitonic properties, the thermalization dynamics of IXs, particularly within moiré superlattices, remain …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166902] Published Fri Apr 24, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Ultrafast Moiré-Resolved Spectroscopy of Interlayer-Exciton Thermalization in Twisted WSe2/WS2 Heterobilayers
item.dc:creator: Jinjae Kim, Hyeonho Lee, Claudia Gollner, et al.

item.title: [7] Emergent Chirality and Enantiomeric Selectivity in Layered NbOX2 Crystals
item.description:
Last authors: Ion Errea, Maia G. Vergniory

Authors: Martin Gutierrez-Amigo, Claudia Felser, Ion Errea, Maia G. Vergniory

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Study of layered crystals with emergent chirality, with STS hint, relevant to electronic structure.

Tags: #STM/STS #2Dmaterials #electronicstructure

Abstract: Author(s): Martin Gutierrez-Amigo, Claudia Felser, Ion Errea, and Maia G. Vergniory Theorists have identified a phase that could facilitate the switching of a crystal between its right-handed and left-handed versions. [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166605] Published Thu Apr 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Martin Gutierrez-Amigo, Claudia Felser, Ion Errea, and Maia G. Vergniory

Theorists have identified a phase that could facilitate the switching of a crystal between its right-handed and left-handed versions.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166605] Published Thu Apr 23, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Emergent Chirality and Enantiomeric Selectivity in Layered NbOX2 Crystals
item.dc:creator: Martin Gutierrez-Amigo, Claudia Felser, Ion Errea, Maia G. Vergniory
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: [8] Orbital Magnetization of Correlated States in Twisted Bilayer Transition Metal Dichalcogenides
item.description:
Last authors: Ting Cao, Di Xiao

Authors: Xiaoyu Liu, Chong Wang, Haoran Chen, Xiao-Wei Zhang, Ting Cao, Di Xiao

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory of orbital magnetization in correlated twisted bilayer TMDs, relevant to user's broader moiré interests.

Tags: #moire #twistedbilayer #stronglycorrelated #2Dmaterials

Abstract: Author(s): Xiaoyu Liu, Chong Wang, Haoran Chen, Xiao-Wei Zhang, Ting Cao, and Di Xiao The modern theory of orbital magnetization, derived for noninteracting electrons, applied to strongly correlated states remains valid for Hartree–Fock states, so can be used to interpret magnetization in interacting moiré systems. [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166606] Published Thu Apr 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xiaoyu Liu, Chong Wang, Haoran Chen, Xiao-Wei Zhang, Ting Cao, and Di Xiao

The modern theory of orbital magnetization, derived for noninteracting electrons, applied to strongly correlated states remains valid for Hartree–Fock states, so can be used to interpret magnetization in interacting moiré systems.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166606] Published Thu Apr 23, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Orbital Magnetization of Correlated States in Twisted Bilayer Transition Metal Dichalcogenides
item.dc:creator: Xiaoyu Liu, Chong Wang, Haoran Chen, Xiao-Wei Zhang, Ting Cao, Di Xiao

item.title: [7] Self-Induced Floquet States via Three-Wave Processes in Synthetic Antiferromagnets
item.description:
Last authors: Thibaut Devolder, Joo-Von Kim

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Floquet engineering in synthetic antiferromagnets, aligning with user's interest in Floquet on real materials.

Tags: #Floquetengineering #antiferromagnetism

Abstract: Author(s): Thibaut Devolder and Joo-Von Kim We present a mechanism for self-induced Floquet states involving acoustic and optical modes in synthetic antiferromagnets. By driving optical modes off-resonantly with radio frequency fields in the canted antiferromagnetic state, limit cycles arising from the predator-prey dynamics of the acoustic a… [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166702] Published Thu Apr 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Thibaut Devolder and Joo-Von Kim

We present a mechanism for self-induced Floquet states involving acoustic and optical modes in synthetic antiferromagnets. By driving optical modes off-resonantly with radio frequency fields in the canted antiferromagnetic state, limit cycles arising from the predator-prey dynamics of the acoustic a…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 166702] Published Thu Apr 23, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Self-Induced Floquet States via Three-Wave Processes in Synthetic Antiferromagnets
item.dc:creator: Thibaut Devolder, Joo-Von Kim
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
